summary.thesis: The strongest outcome comes from framing fall planning as shared family relief, with LinkedIn outperforming lower-context channels for saves and comments.
summary.recommended_action: Publish the practical-relief variant first, then adapt the same thesis into a shorter Instagram caption and checklist CTA.

scenarios.label: practical-relief-resonates
scenarios.narrative: Caregivers engage when the message emphasizes a lighter mental load and one simple planning step.
drivers: Checklist CTA gives readers an immediate next step.

scenarios.label: too-many-tasks-feels-heavy
scenarios.narrative: Engagement softens if the content reads like another burden rather than a simplification tool.
drivers: Dense copy can amplify caregiver fatigue.
Long lists without reassurance feel demanding.

scenarios.label: generic-wellness-underperforms
scenarios.narrative: Generic encouragement without operational detail is least likely to create saves, shares, or follow-through.
drivers: Audience already sees generic encouragement elsewhere.
